content.content:
1	The Bellow Readme, Belongs To The Compressed Folder named dotfiles.zip, and it has been Renamed Form Dotfiles_Tools to -> dotfiles  
2	
3	# dotfiles
4	
5	> A comprehensive dotfiles collection for Linux/macOS development environment with modern CLI tools, TUI applications, and shell configurations.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## 📋 INDEX
10	
11	1. [What's Included](#whats-included)
12	2. [Directory Structure](#directory-structure)
13	3. [Installation](#installation)
14	4. [Tool Dependencies](#tool-dependencies)
15	5. [Quick Start](#quick-start)
16	6. [Aliases Reference](#aliases-reference)
17	7. [Configuration](#configuration)
18	
19	---
20	
21	## 📦 What's Included
22	
23	| Category | Tools |
24	|----------|-------|
25	| **Shells** | Zsh + Oh My Zsh, Fish Shell, Bash |
26	| **History** | Atuin (sync & search) |
27	| **File Management** | eza, yazi |
28	| **File Viewing** | bat, fzf |
29	| **System Monitoring** | btop, fastfetch |
30	| **Git Tools** | lazygit, delta |
31	| **Docker Tools** | lazydocker |
32	| **Directory Jumping** | zoxide |
33	| **Prompts** | Powerlevel10k, Starship |
34	| **Terminal Multiplexer** | tmux |
35	
36	---
37	
38	## 📁 Directory Structure
39	
40	```
41	dotfiles/
42	├── install.sh           # Installation script
43	├── README.md           # This file
44	├── zsh/
45	│   └── .zshrc         # Zsh configuration
46	├── fish/
47	│   └── config.fish    # Fish shell configuration
48	├── bash/
49	│   └── .bashrc       # Bash configuration
50	├── git/
51	│   └── .gitconfig    # Git configuration
52	├── tmux/
53	│   └── .tmux.conf    # Tmux configuration
54	├── starship/
55	│   └── starship.toml  # Starship prompt configuration
56	├── yazi/
57	│   └── yazi.toml     # Yazi file manager config
58	└── bin/
59	                      # Custom scripts (if any)
60	```
61	
62	---
63	
64	## 🔧 Installation
65	
66	### Automated
67	
68	```bash
69	# Clone this repository
70	git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/dotfiles.git ~/dotfiles
71	
72	# Run installation script
73	cd ~/dotfiles
74	./install.sh
75	```
76	
77	### Manual
78	
79	```bash
80	# Clone this repository
81	git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/dotfiles.git ~/dotfiles
82	
83	# Link configuration files
84	ln -sf ~/dotfiles/zsh/.zshrc ~/.zshrc
85	ln -sf ~/dotfiles/bash/.bashrc ~/.bashrc
86	ln -sf ~/dotfiles/git/.gitconfig ~/.gitconfig
87	ln -sf ~/dotfiles/fish/config.fish ~/.config/fish/config.fish
88	ln -sf ~/dotfiles/tmux/.tmux.conf ~/.tmux.conf
89	ln -sf ~/dotfiles/starship/starship.toml ~/.config/starship.toml
90	ln -sf ~/dotfiles/yazi/yazi.toml ~/.config/yazi/yazi.toml
91	
92	# Restart terminal
93	exec $SHELL
94	```
95	
96	---
97	
98	## 🛠 Tool Dependencies
99	
100	### Required Tools
101	
102	```bash
103	# Install via package manager (Ubuntu/Debian)
104	sudo apt install zsh git curl wget
105	
106	# Install via package manager (macOS)
107	brew install zsh git curl wget
108	```
109	
110	### CLI Tools
111	
112	```bash
113	# Install eza (modern ls)
114	wget https://github.com/eza-community/eza/releases/download/v0.20.0/eza_v0.20.0_x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz
115	tar xzf eza_v0.20.0_x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz
116	sudo mv eza /usr/local/bin/
117	
118	# Install bat (modern cat)
119	wget https://github.com/sharkdp/bat/releases/download/v0.24.0/bat-v0.24.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz
120	tar xzf bat-v0.24.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz
121	sudo mv bat /usr/local/bin/
122	
123	# Install fzf (fuzzy finder)
124	git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/junegunn/fzf.git ~/.fzf
125	~/.fzf/install
126	
127	# Install zoxide (smart cd)
128	curl -sSf https://get.zooxide.st | sh
129	
130	# Install starship (prompt)
131	curl -sS https://starship.rs/install.sh | sh
132	
133	# Install yazi (file manager)
134	cargo install yazi --locked
135	
136	# Install btop (system monitor)
137	sudo apt install btop
138	
139	# Install lazygit (git TUI)
140	wget https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazygit/releases/download/v0.45.0/lazygit_v0.45.0_linux_amd64.tar.gz
141	tar xzf lazygit_v0.45.0_linux_amd64.tar.gz
142	sudo mv lazygit /usr/local/bin/
143	
144	# Install fastfetch (system info)
145	wget https://github.com/LinusHenze/fastfetch/releases/download/v2.50.0/fastfetch-linux-amd64.tar.gz
146	tar xzf fastfetch-linux-amd64.tar.gz
147	sudo mv fastfetch /usr/local/bin/
148	```
149	
150	---
151	
152	## ⚡ Quick Start
153	
154	### Switch Shell
155	
156	```bash
157	zsh    # Zsh
158	fish   # Fish
159	bash   # Bash
160	```
161	
162	### Quick Aliases
163	
164	```bash
165	ls/ll/la    # eza aliases
166	cat         # bat
167	yy          # yazi in current directory
168	lg          # lazygit
169	ld          # lazydocker
170	b           # btop
171	ff          # fastfetch
172	```
173	
174	### Tmux
175	
176	```bash
177	tmux                # Start tmux
178	prefix + c           # New window
179	prefix + |           # Split horizontally
180	prefix + -           # Split vertically
181	prefix + h/j/k/l    # Navigate panes
182	prefix + d           # Detach
183	```
184	
185	---
186	
187	## 🔑 Aliases Reference
188	
189	### File Management
190	
191	| Alias | Command |
192	|-------|---------|
193	| `ls` | `eza -la --icons --git` |
194	| `ll` | `eza -l --icons --git` |
195	| `la` | `eza -la --icons` |
196	| `lt` | `eza -lTg` |
197	| `y` | `yazi` |
198	| `yy` | `yazi .` |
199	
200	### Git
201	
202	| Alias | Command |
203	|-------|---------|
204	| `g` | `lazygit` |
205	| `gs` | `git status` |
206	| `ga` | `git add` |
207	| `gc` | `git commit` |
208	| `gp` | `git push` |
209	| `gl` | `git pull` |
210	| `gd` | `git diff` |
211	| `gco` | `git checkout` |
212	| `gb` | `git branch` |
213	| `gf` | `git fetch` |
214	| `gm` | `git merge` |
215	| `gr` | `git rebase` |
216	
217	### Docker
218	
219	| Alias | Command |
220	|-------|---------|
221	| `d` | `docker` |
222	| `dc` | `docker-compose` |
223	| `dcu` | `docker-compose up -d` |
224	| `dcd` | `docker-compose down` |
225	| `dps` | `docker ps` |
226	| `di` | `docker images` |
227	| `dex` | `docker exec -it` |
228	
229	### Navigation
230	
231	| Alias | Command |
232	|-------|---------|
233	| `..` | `cd ..` |
234	| `...` | `cd ../..` |
235	| `~~` | `cd ~` |
236	
237	### System
238	
239	| Alias | Command |
240	|-------|---------|
241	| `b` | `btop` |
242	| `h` | `htop` |
243	| `ff` | `fastfetch` |
244	
245	---
246	
247	## ⚙️ Configuration
248	
249	### Oh My Zsh
250	
251	- Edit `~/.zshrc` to modify plugins
252	- Run `p10k configure` to configure Powerlevel10k
253	
254	### Starship
255	
256	- Edit `~/.config/starship.toml` to customize prompt
257	- Theme: Catppuccin Mocha
258	
259	### Tmux
260	
261	- Prefix: `Ctrl+a`
262	- Run `tmux` to start
263	- Configuration is in `.tmux.conf`
264	
265	### Git
266	
267	- Edit `~/.gitconfig` to set your name and email
268	
269	---
270	
271	## 📚 References
272	
273	| Tool | URL |
274	|------|-----|
275	| Oh My Zsh | https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh |
276	| Powerlevel10k | https://github.com/romkatv/powerlevel10k |
277	| Starship | https://starship.rs |
278	| Yazi | https://yazi-rs.github.io |
279	| Lazygit | https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazygit |
280	| Eza | https://github.com/eza-community/eza |
281	| Bat | https://github.com/sharkdp/bat |
282	| Fzf | https://github.com/junegunn/fzf |
283	| Zoxide | https://github.com/ajeetdsouza/zoxide |
284	
285	---
286	
287	## 📝 License
288	
289	MIT License - Feel free to use and customize!
290	
291	---
292	
293	**Last Updated:** April 2026
294	**Version:** 2.0
295

1	# Dotfiles Consolidation — Migration Plan ✅ COMPLETED
2	
3	> **Status: ✅ ALL FEATURES MIGRATED**
4	> All 3 source repos have been fully merged into `packages/`. The old repos have been deleted.
5	> This file is kept as historical reference. For current workflow, see `GITOPS-WORKFLOW.md`.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Goal
10	
11	Merge features from 3 separate dotfiles projects into a single, unified dotfiles repository managed by GNU Stow.
12	
13	---
14	
15	## Source Codebases (Historical)
16	
17	| # | Project | Remote | Manager | Shells | Theme | Status |
18	|---|---------|--------|---------|--------|-------|--------|
19	| 1 | `dotfile.d/` | `Sabir-test/dotfiles` | GNU Stow | Bash | None | ✅ Merged, source deleted |
20	| 2 | `dotfiles/` | `Sabir-test/dotfiles` | `ln -sf` + Stow (staging) | Zsh+Fish+Bash | Catppuccin Mocha | ✅ Merged, source deleted |
21	| 3 | `windows-wsl-terminal-customization/` | `Sabir-test/windows-wsl-terminal-customization` | `ln -sf` | Bash (Oh-My-Bash) | Gruvbox Dark | ✅ Merged, source deleted |
22	
23	---
24	
25	## Key Decisions (Defaults)
26	
27	| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
28	|----------|--------|-----------|
29	| Dotfiles manager | GNU Stow | Cleaner than manual symlinks; already proven in `dotfile.d/` |
30	| Theme | Catppuccin Mocha | Richer shell + TUI integration; already in `dotfiles/` |
31	| Package layout | Flat at root (`bash/`, `tmux/`, `zsh/`, etc.) | Matches existing `dotfile.d/` convention |
32	| Remote | `github.com/Sabir-test/dotfiles` | Already shared by `dotfile.d/` + `dotfiles/` |
33	| Old repos | Deleted after full migration | Clean workspace, no stale references |
34	
35	---
36	
37	## Final Package Layout
38	
39	```
40	packages/
41	├── agent/        — AI agent governance rules
42	├── bash/         — .bashrc, .profile (merged from all 3 sources)
43	├── bin/          — custom scripts
44	├── cursor/       — Cursor editor settings
45	├── fish/         — config.fish
46	├── gh/           — GitHub CLI config
47	├── git/          — .gitconfig (merged from all sources)
48	├── pam/          — .pam_environment locale
49	├── ripgrep/      — ripgreprc
50	├── scripts/      — deploy, helper scripts
51	├── starship/     — starship.toml (Catppuccin base)
52	├── tmux/         — .tmux.conf (merged)
53	├── vscode/       — VS Code settings
54	├── windows-terminal/ — Windows Terminal settings
55	├── yazi/         — yazi config
56	└── zsh/          — .zshrc
57	```
58	
59	Additional files at repo root:
60	
61	- `install.sh` — orchestrator: tools → Stow deploy → tmux plugins → Docker network
62	- `scripts/install-tools.sh` — idempotent 12-phase tool installer (Linux/macOS)
63	- `scripts/deploy-configs.sh` — Stow wrapper for config deployment
64	
65	---
66	
67	## Steps Completed (Historical Record)
68	
69	### Step 1-4: Initialize repo, create packages, extract files ✅
70	
71	### Step 5: Merge duplicate configs ✅
72	
73	### Step 6: Create unified bootstrap ✅
74	
75	### Step 7: Verification ✅
76	
77	### Old repo deletion ✅
78	

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

1	# AGENTS.md
2	
3	## What this repo is
4	
5	A **migration workspace** that consolidates 3 separate dotfiles repositories into a single unified dotfiles repo managed by GNU Stow. This is NOT a working dotfiles deployment — it is the factory floor.
6	
7	## Source repositories (gitignored, keep them that way)
8	
9	| Repo | Manager | Theme | Shells | Key features |
10	|------|---------|-------|--------|-------------|
11	| `dotfile.d/` | GNU Stow (7 packages) | None | Bash | VM infra, editor configs, PAM locale, agent governance |
12	| `dotfiles/` | `ln -sf` (+ Stow staging zip) | Catppuccin Mocha | Zsh+Fish+Bash | Rich TUI tools (lazygit, yazi, btop, tmux), modern CLIs, Starship |
13	| `windows-wsl-terminal-customization/` | `ln -sf` | Gruvbox Dark | Bash (Oh-My-Bash) | Windows Terminal, 50+ aliases, TPM, ripgreprc, 2-script bootstrap |
14	
15	All three are *references*. Never `git add`, submodule, or delete them.
16	
17	## Migration state (current)
18	
19	- Root repo: 5 commits (init plan → workflow doc → fix submodule pointers → gitignore → initial migration)
20	- `packages/` directory has 16 packages created from initial migration
21	- The old repos sit untracked per `.gitignore` (which excludes them as `/path/`)
22	
23	## Canonical docs
24	
25	Read these first before any migration work:
26	
27	1. **`MIGRATION-PLAN.md`** — what to merge, in what order, merge strategies per file
28	2. **`GITOPS-WORKFLOW.md`** — branch strategy, commit convention, pre-commit verification
29	
30	## Key decisions (from MIGRATION-PLAN.md)
31	
32	- **Dotfiles manager**: GNU Stow (not `ln -sf`)
33	- **Theme**: Catppuccin Mocha (port useful modules from Gruvbox)
34	- **Package layout**: Flat at root (`bash/`, `tmux/`, `zsh/`, etc.)
35	- **Remote**: `github.com/Sabir-test/dotfiles` (already shared by 2 of 3 repos)
36	
37	## Branch workflow
38	
39	| Branch | Purpose |
40	|--------|---------|
41	| `MAIN` | Stable, deployable milestones. Merge only via `--no-ff`. |
42	| `MIGRATION` | Active daily work. Default branch for agent. |
43	| `FEATURE/<package>` | For complex per-package work, branch from `MIGRATION`. |
44	
45	## Commit convention
46	
47	```
48	<type>: <short description>
49	
50	[body — conflicts resolved, decisions made]
51	```
52	
53	Types: `migrate`, `merge`, `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `refactor`, `chore`. One commit per logical move. See GITOPS-WORKFLOW.md for examples.
54	
55	## Pre-commit verification (from GITOPS-WORKFLOW.md)
56	
57	1. Git status clean — no accidental submodule pointers or dirty child repos
58	2. Stow packages are valid — directory structure mirrors `$HOME` path
59	3. Configs pass syntax check: `bash -n`, `zsh -n`, `tmux -c "list-keys"`, etc.
60	4. `.gitignore` covers old repos — confirm they show as `!!` ignored
61	
62	## Target package layout (from MIGRATION-PLAN.md)
63	
64	```
65	packages/
66	├── agent/        bash/         bin/           cursor/
67	├── fish/         gh/           git/           pam/
68	├── ripgrep/      scripts/      starship/      tmux/
69	├── vscode/       windows-terminal/  yazi/     zsh/
70	```
71	
72	Each mirrors the `$HOME` path so Stow creates correct symlinks (e.g. `git/.gitconfig` → `~/.gitconfig`).
73	
74	## Verification commands
75	
76	```bash
77	# Syntax checks
78	bash -n packages/bash/.bashrc
79	zsh -n packages/zsh/.zshrc
80	tmux -c "list-keys"  # loads .tmux.conf implicitly
81	
82	# Stow dry-run (from repo root)
83	stow --simulate --target="$HOME" packages/*
84	
85	# Full deploy
86	stow --restow --target="$HOME" packages/*
87	```
88	
89	## Things to never do
90	
91	- Commit SSH keys, GitHub tokens, `hosts.yml`, or `*.pem`
92	- Commit `node_modules`, `*.log`, `.DS_Store`
93	- Add old repos as submodules or track them directly
94	- Force-push to `MAIN`
95	

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

1	# Scope: dotfiles — Shell & CLI Tool Configuration
2	
3	**All shell environment configs, managed with GNU Stow.**
4	
5	## What belongs here
6	
7	- Shell configs: `.zshrc`, `config.fish`, `.bashrc`
8	- Terminal multiplexer: `.tmux.conf`
9	- Prompt: `starship.toml`, Powerlevel10k config
10	- Git: `.gitconfig`
11	- CLI tools: yazi, btop, lazygit, atuin, fzf, zoxide, eza, bat, lazydocker
12	- Editor configs (non-project-specific)
13	- `install.sh` bootstrap script
14	
15	## Target structure (tracked files, not zipped)
16	
17	```
18	dotfiles/
19	├── install.sh
20	├── zsh/.zshrc
21	├── fish/config.fish
22	├── bash/.bashrc
23	├── git/.gitconfig
24	├── tmux/.tmux.conf
25	├── starship/starship.toml
26	├── yazi/yazi.toml
27	└── bin/          # custom scripts
28	```
29	
30	## Action required
31	
32	The current `Dotfiles.zip` should be **extracted and each file tracked individually** in this repo.
33	This makes diffs meaningful and allows per-file history.
34	
35	```bash
36	# Extract and reorganise:
37	unzip Dotfiles.zip -d dotfiles-extracted/
38	# Then move each config into its Stow package directory above
39	```
40	
41	Deploy on a fresh VM:
42	```bash
43	git clone https://github.com/Sabir-test/dotfiles.git ~/dotfiles
44	cd ~/dotfiles && ./install.sh
45	# install.sh should run: stow zsh fish bash git tmux starship yazi
46	```
47	
48	## What does NOT belong here
49	
50	- Project-specific editor configs → stay in project `.vscode/` or `.editorconfig`
51	- Homelab/infrastructure configs → `home-template`
52	- VM-specific app installs → `sandboxed-devspace`
53	
54	## Used by
55	
56	`sandboxed-devspace` references this repo for VM bootstrap:
57	```bash
58	~/dotfiles/install.sh
59	```
60	

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

1	# GitOps Workflow — Dotfiles Repo
2	
3	## Repository Structure
4	
5	```
6	dotfiles-projects/             ← single source of truth
7	├── GITOPS-WORKFLOW.md
8	├── .gitignore
9	├── install.sh
10	├── .githooks/                  ← pre-commit hooks
11	├── .github/workflows/          ← CI pipeline
12	├── packages/
13	│   ├── agent/          bash/           bin/           cursor/
14	│   ├── fish/           gh/             git/           pam/
15	│   ├── ripgrep/        scripts/        starship/      tmux/
16	│   ├── vscode/         windows-terminal/  yazi/       zsh/
17	└── .omo/
18	    ├── drafts/          ← WIP documents
19	    ├── plans/           ← execution plans
20	    ├── sessions/        ← session exports
21	    └── skills/          ← opencode skill definitions
22	```
23	
24	---
25	
26	## Branching Strategy
27	
28	```
29	MAIN  ●──────●──────●──────●────  (stable, deployable)
30	```
31	
32	Only one branch. All work happens on `MAIN`. No feature branches, no migration branches — the migration is complete, and this repo is now in maintenance/iteration mode.
33	
34	If you need to experiment with a risky change, commit on `MAIN` but do not push until stable, or use a local branch that you squash-merge back.
35	
36	---
37	
38	## Commit Convention
39	
40	```
41	<type>: <short description>
42	
43	[optional body — details on conflicts resolved, decisions made]
44	```
45	
46	### Types
47	
48	| Type | When to use |
49	|------|-------------|
50	| `feat` | Adding a new config, package, or feature |
51	| `fix` | Fixing a broken config, path, or syntax |
52	| `docs` | Changes to docs, README, workflow |
53	| `refactor` | Restructuring packages, renaming, reorganizing |
54	| `chore` | .gitignore, git config, tooling, CI, hooks |
55	
56	### Examples
57	
58	```
59	feat: add macOS Homebrew paths to bash/.bashrc
60	fix: correct neovim binary name in bash aliases
61	docs: update AGENTS.md with current package count
62	refactor: rename packages/scripts to packages/bin
63	chore: add pre-commit hooks for Stow validation
64	```
65	
66	---
67	
68	## Verification Before Each Commit
69	
70	1. **Git status is clean** — no untracked secrets, no dirty submodules
71	2. **Stow packages are valid** — `stow --simulate --target="$HOME" packages/*` succeeds
72	3. **Configs syntax-check** — `bash -n`, `zsh -n`, `tmux -c "list-keys"`, etc. on changed files
73	4. **CI passes** — GitHub Actions validates on push
74	
75	---
76	
77	## Remote
78	
79	```
80	origin  github.com/Sabir-test/dotfiles
81	```
82	
83	Push to `MAIN` directly. If the push introduces a regression, fix it in the next commit — no revert-and-branch ceremony needed for a personal dotfiles repo.
84

content.content:
1	# VM 200 Agent Governance — KDE Neon Guest
2	
3	> **You are running inside a Virtual Machine (VM 200) on a Proxmox host.**
4	> You are NOT on bare metal. You are NOT on the Proxmox host.
5	> Never confuse guest-side and host-side operations.
6	
7	## 1. Architecture Awareness
8	
9	This machine is a **KDE Neon (Ubuntu-based) VM** running on Proxmox VE.
10	The physical host is an ASUS TUF Gaming F15 (FX507ZC4) with i5-12500H / 32GB RAM.
11	
12	- **Virtualization**: QEMU/KVM with PCI passthrough (full Physical Function, NOT SR-IOV)
13	- **Why hardware looks real**: PCI passthrough exposes physical devices directly to this VM.
14	  `lspci` will show real Intel/NVIDIA hardware — this does NOT mean you are on bare metal.
15	- **Host kernel**: `6.14.8-2-pve` (Proxmox) — you will NEVER see this kernel inside the VM
16	- **Guest kernel**: Ubuntu generic (e.g., `6.14.0-29-generic`) — this is YOUR kernel
17	- **Host IP**: 10.42.7.2 (not directly accessible from here)
18	- **Guest IP**: 10.42.7.9 (static, on vmbr1 bridge)
19	
20	## 2. Kernel Rules
21	
22	| Rule | Explanation |
23	|------|-------------|
24	| **Never recommend installing PVE kernels** | `proxmox-headers-*`, `pve-kernel-*` are host-only packages. They do not belong here. |
25	| **Never recommend switching to a PVE kernel** | This VM runs Ubuntu generic kernels. That is correct and intentional. |
26	| **Kernel upgrades are OK** | You may upgrade within Ubuntu generic kernels (e.g., 6.14 → 6.15) if needed. |
27	| **Kernel pinning is OK** | The VM pins to `6.14.0-29-generic` for stability. Respect the pin. |
28	
29	## 3. GPU & Driver Rules
30	
31	### Hardware Map (Guest PCI → Physical Device)
32	
33	| Guest PCI ID | Device | Physical Hardware | Drives |
34	|:---|:---|:---|:---|
35	| `01:00.0` | Intel Iris Xe Graphics `[8086:46a6]` | Physical Intel iGPU | Built-in laptop eDP screen |
36	| `02:00.0` | NVIDIA RTX 3050 Mobile `[10de:25a2]` | Physical NVIDIA dGPU | HDMI external monitor |
37	| `03:00.0` | Intel Alder Lake PCH-P Audio `[8086:51c8]` | Physical audio controller | Built-in speakers & mic |
38	| `00:1b.0` | ICH9 HD Audio `[8086:293e]` | QEMU virtual audio | Virtual audio (noVNC/SPICE) |
39	
40	### Driver Rules
41	
42	| Rule | Explanation |
43	|------|-------------|
44	| **Do NOT install SR-IOV drivers** | This VM uses full PF passthrough. SR-IOV (`i915-sriov`) is a host-side concept that does not apply here. |
45	| **Do NOT modify VFIO bindings** | VFIO is a host-side mechanism. This VM does not use VFIO. |
46	| **i915 driver is correct** | The Intel iGPU uses the standard `i915` kernel module. This is normal. |
47	| **nvidia proprietary driver is correct** | The NVIDIA GPU uses the proprietary `nvidia` driver. DKMS builds it for the guest kernel. |
48	| **xe driver is blacklisted** | `/etc/modprobe.d/blacklist-xe.conf` — intentional, do not undo. |
49	
50	## 4. Known False Positives
51	
52	These conditions LOOK like errors but are **completely normal** in this passthrough VM:
53	
54	| What You See | Why It Is Normal |
55	|:---|:---|
56	| `i915: VBT not found` in dmesg | Expected — the iGPU VBIOS table is not fully exposed through passthrough. Display works fine regardless. |
57	| Kernel taint flags from NVIDIA | Standard for proprietary nvidia module. Not a problem. |
58	| PCI addresses don't match "expected" bare-metal slots | Passthrough remaps PCI topology. Guest addresses (01:00.0, 02:00.0) are QEMU-assigned, not physical. |
59	| `i915` loads on PCI 01:00.0 instead of 00:02.0 | Normal for passthrough — bare-metal iGPU sits at 00:02.0 but QEMU remaps it. |
60	| No `nouveau` driver loaded | Intentional — proprietary `nvidia` driver is used instead. |
61	| `snd_hda_intel` on two different devices | One is real passthrough audio (03:00.0), one is QEMU virtual (00:1b.0). Both are correct. |
62	
63	## 5. Scope Boundaries
64	
65	### You CAN Do (Guest-Side)
66	- Install/remove packages via apt
67	- Configure display settings (KDE Display Configuration)
68	- Manage i915 and nvidia drivers (guest kernel modules)
69	- Edit `/etc/default/grub` and run `update-grub` (guest bootloader)
70	- Configure network (NetworkManager, static IP 10.42.7.9)
71	- Manage desktop environment (KDE Plasma, SDDM)
72	- Install Flatpaks, Snaps, AppImages
73	- Configure PipeWire/PulseAudio
74	- Run CUDA/AI workloads on the NVIDIA GPU
75	- Manage systemd services inside the VM
76	- Create/edit files in /home/mbs
77	
78	### You CANNOT Do (Host-Side — Do NOT Attempt)
79	- Modify the Proxmox host kernel or boot parameters
80	- Change VFIO/passthrough bindings
81	- Install SR-IOV or DKMS modules meant for the host
82	- Access `/etc/pve/` or Proxmox cluster configuration
83	- Sleep/hibernate the physical laptop (only VM shutdown/reboot)
84	- Modify IOMMU groups or PCI device assignments
85	- Access other VMs or containers on the host
86	
87	## 6. Communicating Host-Side Issues
88	
89	If you identify a problem that requires host-side intervention:
90	
91	1. **Do NOT attempt to fix it from inside the VM**
92	2. Write a report to `/home/mbs/HostReports/` with:
93	   - Filename: `YYYY-MM-DD_description.md`
94	   - What you observed (symptoms, logs, error messages)
95	   - What you believe the root cause is
96	   - What host-side action you recommend
97	3. Inform the user that the issue requires host-side attention
98	
99	## 7. Display Configuration
100	
101	- **SDDM (login screen)**: X11-based, uses `/etc/X11/xorg.conf.d/10-gpu-priority.conf`
102	- **User session**: KWin Wayland (ignores xorg.conf, uses DRM/KMS directly)
103	- **Primary display**: Intel iGPU → eDP laptop panel
104	- **Secondary display**: NVIDIA → HDMI external
105	- **Virtual display**: virtio-gpu via noVNC (accessible as Virtual-1 in KDE Display Settings)
106	
107	## 8. Network
108	
109	- **IP**: 10.42.7.9 (static via NetworkManager)
110	- **Gateway**: 10.42.7.1 (OPNsense VM 100)
111	- **DNS**: Managed by OPNsense
112	- **SSH**: Enabled on port 22
113	
114	## 9. System Services to Preserve
115	
116	- `sddm` — display manager
117	- `NetworkManager` — network (do NOT switch to systemd-networkd)
118	- `pipewire` / `wireplumber` — audio stack
119	- `qemu-guest-agent` — host communication (critical for `qm guest exec`)
120	- `ssh` — remote access
121	
122	---
123	*This document is the single source of truth for AI agents running inside VM 200.*
124	*Last updated: 2026-03-15*
125	

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

content.text:
Based on my analysis of the documentation in this dotfiles repo, here's my comprehensive report:

---

## Documentation Status Report

### 1. DOCUMENTATION INVENTORY

| File | Status | Relevance | Last Updated |
|------|--------|-----------|--------------|
| **README.md** | Outdated/Generic | Low | April 2026 (template-like) |
| **AGENTS.md** | Partially Outdated | Medium | Migration info, but contradicts workflow |
| **GITOPS-WORKFLOW.md** | Current | High | Reflects actual operation mode |
| **MIGRATION-PLAN.md** | Historical | Low | Correctly marked COMPLETED; use for reference |
| **SCOPE.md** | Partially Outdated | Medium | Scope accurate but extraction already done |
| **UserNotes.md** | Unclear | Very Low | Personal notes, minimal substance |
| **AGENT_VM.md** | Current | High | VM governance, recently updated (2026-03) |

---

## 2. CRITICAL CONTRADICTIONS IDENTIFIED

### Contradiction #1: Migration State
- **AGENTS.md** says: "old repos sit untracked per `.gitignore` (which excludes them as `/path/`)"
- **MIGRATION-PLAN.md** says: "✅ ALL FEATURES MIGRATED... old repos have been **deleted**"
- **GITOPS-WORKFLOW.md** says: migration is "complete" → repo in "maintenance/iteration mode"
- **Reality** (from git log): Old repos were deleted in early commits; `.gitignore` entries are stale remnants

### Contradiction #2: Branching Strategy
- **AGENTS.md** describes: `MAIN`, `MIGRATION`, `FEATURE/<package>` branches with specific workflows
- **GITOPS-WORKFLOW.md** says: "Only one branch. All work happens on `MAIN`... No feature branches"
- **Impact**: Developer confusion about which workflow to follow

### Contradiction #3: File Extraction
- **SCOPE.md** says: "current `Dotfiles.zip` should be **extracted** and tracked individually"
- **MIGRATION-PLAN.md** says: extraction is ✅ COMPLETED in Step 1-4
- **Reality** (git log commit `abee307`): Extraction already done; old zips removed in `b750704`

### Contradiction #4: Installation Instructions
- **README.md** has placeholder: `git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/dotfiles.git`
- **AGENTS.md** specifies actual remote: `github.com/Sabir-test/dotfiles`
- **Reality**: Actual remote confirmed in GITOPS-WORKFLOW.md

---

## 3. DOCUMENTATION GAPS (MISSING)

### Critical Gaps

1. **CLAUDE.md / Project Instructions** ❌
   - No `.claude/instructions.md` or project initialization file
   - Agent/user unfamiliar with repo has no single source of truth
   - **Needed**: High-level project orientation document

2. **Stow Architecture & Rationale** ❌
   - No explanation of why Stow was chosen over alternatives
   - No diagram showing how `packages/bash/.bashrc` → `~/.bashrc` via Stow
   - New contributors won't understand the model
   - **Needed**: Architecture decision document (ADR)

3. **Deployment / Setup Guide** ❌
   - README.md has generic steps but doesn't clarify this is a **consolidated** repo
   - No step-by-step for: clone → install tools → Stow → verify
   - No troubleshooting for Stow conflicts (if ~/.bashrc already exists)
   - **Needed**: "Quick Start" → "Production Deployment" guide

4. **Package Maintenance Workflow** ❌
   - How to add a new package? (directory structure, Stow rules, CI check)
   - How to modify existing config files?
   - How to handle conflicts between multiple shells (bash/zsh/fish)?
   - **Needed**: "Adding/Updating Packages" guide

5. **Pre-commit Hooks & CI/CD** ❌
   - GITOPS-WORKFLOW.md mentions "pre-commit hooks" verification but `.githooks/` is empty
   - GitHub Actions mentioned ("CI passes") but workflow not documented
   - **Needed**: `.github/workflows/` documentation + hook implementation guide

6. **Relationship to Other Repos** ❌
   - SCOPE.md mentions `home-template`, `sandboxed-devspace` but no explanation
   - How does this dotfiles repo integrate with those?
   - **Needed**: "Ecosystem Map" diagram/doc

7. **Agent Governance & Skill Documentation** ❌
   - `.opencode/skills/` directory exists (4 skill definitions) but not integrated into docs
   - AGENT_VM.md is specific to VM 200 but no general agent framework documentation
   - **Needed**: "Agent Framework" + "Skill Development Guide"

8. **Configuration Customization** ❌
   - Which configs are "base" vs "customizable"?
   - How to fork/personalize without breaking Stow tracking?
   - How to diff against upstream changes?
   - **Needed**: "Customization & Forking" guide

9. **Troubleshooting Guide** ❌
   - Stow symlink conflicts
   - Syntax errors in shell configs
   - Missing tool dependencies
   - **Needed**: Common issues + resolution

10. **Commit Examples** ❌
    - GITOPS-WORKFLOW.md shows commit types but no real examples
    - Need before/after illustrations
    - **Needed**: "Commit Examples" section with actual diffs

---

## 4. OUTDATED/MISLEADING CONTENT

| Issue | File | Severity |
|-------|------|----------|
| Generic template placeholders | README.md | Medium |
| Describes branch workflow that no longer exists | AGENTS.md | High |
| Misleading about old repo status | AGENTS.md + SCOPE.md | Medium |
| `.gitignore` still lists old repo paths but they're deleted | (implicit) | Low |
| docs/ folder empty (created but unused) | Repository structure | Low |
| UserNotes.md unintelligible (grammar/clarity issues) | UserNotes.md | Low |

---

## 5. RECOMMENDED DOCUMENTATION STRUCTURE

```
dotfiles-projects/
├── README.md                 (rewrite: executive summary + quick links)
├── docs/
│   ├── ARCHITECTURE.md       (Stow design, package layout, rationale)
│   ├── QUICK-START.md        (30-second clone & deploy)
│   ├── SETUP.md              (detailed deployment for new users)
│   ├── PACKAGES.md           (each package: purpose, key configs, customization)
│   ├── ADDING-PACKAGES.md    (how to create new packages, Stow rules, CI checks)
│   ├── MAINTENANCE.md        (updating configs, handling conflicts, shell-specific notes)
│   ├── TROUBLESHOOTING.md    (common issues, Stow conflicts, syntax checks)
│   ├── CI-CD.md              (GitHub Actions workflows, pre-commit hooks)
│   ├── ECOSYSTEM.md          (relationship to home-template, sandboxed-devspace)
│   ├── AGENTS.md             (agent governance framework, skills, VM rules)
│   └── CONTRIBUTING.md       (commit conventions, code review, branch rules)
├── .github/workflows/        (document each workflow)
├── .githooks/               (implement + document hooks)
├── packages/
│   ├── README.md            (overview of all packages)
│   └── [each package]/
│       └── README.md        (package-specific config, customization)
└── CLAUDE.md                (NEW: agent/user orientation)
```

---

## 6. PRIORITY FIX LIST

### Immediate (resolves contradictions)

1. **Delete AGENTS.md or rewrite it** ✏️
   - Option A: Merge into GITOPS-WORKFLOW.md as "Historical Context"
   - Option B: Rewrite to reflect current state (1 MAIN branch, completed migration)
   - Current state: **misleading** to new users

2. **Update README.md** ✏️
   - Remove template placeholders
   - Add links to GITOPS-WORKFLOW.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, QUICK-START.md
   - Clarify: "This is a **unified, consolidated** dotfiles repo managed by GNU Stow"

3. **Archive MIGRATION-PLAN.md properly** ✏️
